sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that DuetServe maintains mean TBT below 150 ms on Azure-Code at QPS=16 with Qwen3-8B while vLLM and SGLang-Chunked exceed 200 ms.

verification.spec: Run DuetServe, vLLM, and SGLang-Chunked on Qwen3-8B Azure-Code at QPS=16. Assert DuetServe mean TBT <= 150 ms AND vLLM mean TBT > 200 ms AND SGLang-Chunked mean TBT > 200 ms.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on Azure-Code at QPS=16 with Qwen3-8B, DuetServe achieves ~13.57 output req/s and SGLang-Default achieves ~12.43 output req/s (1.1x throughput ratio).

verification.spec: Run DuetServe and SGLang-Default on Qwen3-8B Azure-Code at QPS=16. Compute throughput ratio = DuetServe_req_per_s / SGLang_req_per_s; assert ratio >= 1.1 within tolerance; also assert DuetServe ~ 13.57 req/s within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on Mooncake at QPS=5 with Qwen3-8B, DuetServe achieves ~2.72 output req/s vs vLLM ~2.14 (1.3x throughput ratio).

verification.spec: Run DuetServe and vLLM (chunked-prefill, token budget 8192) on Qwen3-8B with Mooncake trace (1000 sampled requests, Poisson arrivals) at QPS=5. Assert DuetServe req/s ~ 2.72 within tolerance and vLLM req/s ~ 2.14 within tolerance; ratio >= 1.25.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on H100, linear-layer FLOPs scale roughly linearly with active TPC count while HBM bandwidth scales super-linearly (saturating earlier).

verification.spec: Using libsmctrl, sweep active TPC counts (e.g., 4, 8, 16, 32, 48, 66) on H100. For each, measure (a) GEMM throughput (TFLOPS) and (b) HBM bandwidth via cudaMemcpy. Fit/check: TFLOPS/peak vs TPC fraction has slope ~1 (linear, R^2 > 0.9), while HBM_bw/peak vs TPC fraction is concave with HBM_bw/peak >= 0.5 by 20% TPCs.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: linear-scaling slope for FLOPs vs TPC fraction

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the linear layer benchmarking on H100 confirms the saturation point near T=8192 tokens for a 4096x4096 linear layer with input dimension Tx4096, as shown in Figure 2(a)

verification.spec: Benchmark a 4096x4096 linear layer with input shape (T, 4096) on H100 for T in {32, 64, ..., 16384}; measure attainable TFLOPS; assert that at T=8192 the achieved TFLOPS is within 20% of the peak measured across all T values (i.e., saturation is reached by T=8192)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that under an 8192-token budget on Qwen3-8B with H100, prefill-only batch execution latency exceeds 180 ms across batch and prompt configurations

verification.spec: Run prefill-only iteration on Qwen3-8B on H100 with token budget 8192 in configurations such as 16x512, 8x1024, 4x2048, 2x4096, 1x8192; measure per-iteration latency; assert minimum latency across configs is >= 180 ms within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that for an 8192-token prefill on Qwen3-8B, the attention module accounts for approximately 25% of the total forward latency

verification.spec: Run a single 1x8192 prefill on Qwen3-8B on H100; profile per-operator latency; compute (attention_latency / total_forward_latency); assert ratio approximately 0.25 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that decode-only batch execution on Qwen3-8B with token budget 8 exhibits more than 4x latency variation as context length increases from 1024 to 16384

verification.spec: Run decode-only iteration on Qwen3-8B on H100 with batch=8 and context lengths {1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384}; measure per-iteration latency; assert max_latency / min_latency >= 4.0 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that profiling on H100 confirms 20% of active SMs achieve approximately 60% of peak HBM bandwidth

verification.spec: Use libsmctrl or equivalent to restrict active TPCs on H100; run cudaMemcpy microbenchmark with ~20% of total SMs active; measure achieved HBM bandwidth and compare to peak; assert achieved/peak >= 0.60 within tolerance

sub_tasks.paper_unit: fraction of peak HBM bandwidth at 20% SMs

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that DuetServe achieves up to 1.3x higher output request throughput than vLLM on the Mooncake workload with Qwen3-8B (TP=1) at QPS=5 (DuetServe 2.72 req/s vs vLLM 2.14 req/s)

verification.spec: Serve 1000 sampled Mooncake requests with Qwen3-8B (TP=1) on H100 under Poisson arrival at QPS=5; run DuetServe and vLLM (chunked prefill, token budget 8192); measure output requests/s; assert DuetServe/vLLM ratio >= 1.25 and DuetServe absolute throughput approximately 2.72 req/s within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that DuetServe maintains TBT below 150 ms at QPS=16 on Azure-Code with Qwen3-8B (TP=1), while vLLM and SGLang-Chunked exceed 200 ms and SGLang-Default exceeds 680 ms

verification.spec: Serve Azure-Code workload with Qwen3-8B (TP=1) on H100 under Poisson arrivals at QPS=16; run DuetServe, vLLM, SGLang-Default, SGLang-Chunked; measure mean TBT; assert DuetServe mean TBT < 150 ms, vLLM and SGLang-Chunked > 200 ms, SGLang-Default > 680 ms (each within 25% tolerance)

sub_tasks.paper_unit: ms (DuetServe TBT upper bound at QPS=16)

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduce paper's reported baselines
Code has been implemented and run such that vLLM-TP2 and SGLang-Chunked-TP2 mean TBT exceeds 200 ms when QPS exceeds 13 on Azure-Code with Qwen3-14B.

verification.spec: Run vLLM (TP=2) and SGLang with enable-mixed-chunk (TP=2) on Qwen3-14B Azure-Code workload at QPS=15. Assert mean TBT for both > 200 ms.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: ms mean TBT (lower bound for baselines)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that SGLang-Default baseline TBT exceeds 680 ms on Azure-Code at QPS=16 with Qwen3-8B (demonstrating unbounded TBT growth from prefill interruptions).

verification.spec: Run SGLang v0.5.0 (default throughput-oriented scheduler, NO enable-mixed-chunk) on Qwen3-8B Azure-Code at QPS=16. Assert mean TBT > 680 ms.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that PD-aggregated vLLM with Qwen3-8B on 2xH100 saturates near QPS=7 while PD-disaggregated Dynamo (1P+1D) saturates near QPS=4 (TTFT rises sharply for QPS>4) on the 8000 input / 200 output token workload

verification.spec: Deploy Agg-vLLM (round-robin on 2 H100s, token budget 8192) and Disagg-Dynamo (1P+1D) with Qwen3-8B; serve a workload of 8000 input tokens and 200 output tokens per request; sweep QPS in {2..10}; assert that Disagg-Dynamo TTFT rises sharply (>2x) at QPS=5 vs QPS=4 and Agg-vLLM TTFT saturates near QPS=7

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Disagg-Dynamo achieves less than half the total tokens per second of Agg-vLLM on the 8000-input/200-output Qwen3-8B workload at saturation

verification.spec: Measure peak total tokens/s for Disagg-Dynamo (1P+1D) and Agg-vLLM with Qwen3-8B on 2 H100s, 8000 input / 200 output tokens per request; assert Disagg-Dynamo throughput / Agg-vLLM throughput <= 0.5 within tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on Azure-Code with Qwen3-14B (TP=2), Dynamo-1P1D achieves lowest TBT but lowest throughput because the prefill GPU becomes the bottleneck, while DuetServe achieves the highest throughput with TBT below 150 ms even under saturation

verification.spec: Serve Azure-Code with Qwen3-14B on 2xH100; run DuetServe-TP2, vLLM-TP2, SGLang-Default-TP2, SGLang-Chunked-TP2, and Dynamo-1P1D under Poisson arrivals at QPS in {7,9,11,13,15,17}; measure mean TBT and output req/s; assert (a) DuetServe peak throughput >= all baselines, (b) DuetServe mean TBT < 150 ms at QPS=17, (c) Dynamo achieves lowest TBT but lowest peak throughput among non-default systems

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Code has been implemented and run such that DuetServe's adaptive scheduler outperforms (in output req/s) every static SM-partition baseline (Sd22-Sp44, Sd33-Sp33, Sd44-Sp22) on at least one workload for Qwen3-8B.

verification.spec: Run DuetServe with adaptive scheduling and with three static partitions (Sd=22/Sp=44, 33/33, 44/22 TPCs) on Mooncake workload (Qwen3-8B, TP=1) at saturation QPS. Assert DuetServe output req/s strictly greater than max of the three static configurations by >=5%.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: x throughput vs best static partition

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that DuetServe's adaptive SM partitioning outperforms all three static partitioning baselines (Sd22-Sp44, Sd33-Sp33, Sd44-Sp22) in output request throughput across Azure-Code, Azure-Conv, and Mooncake workloads on Qwen3-8B (TP=1) and Qwen3-14B (TP=2)

verification.spec: For each of {Azure-Code, Azure-Conv, Mooncake} x {Qwen3-8B TP=1, Qwen3-14B TP=2}, run DuetServe with adaptive partitioning and with static configurations Sd22-Sp44, Sd33-Sp33, Sd44-Sp22 (decode SMs / prefill SMs in TPC units); measure output req/s at a fixed saturating QPS; assert DuetServe throughput >= best static configuration throughput in every workload-model combination, within 10% tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the attention-aware roofline model's predicted latency tracks profiled latency for 8x1024 prefill and 16x1024 decode workloads on Qwen3-8B (TP=1) and Qwen3-14B (TP=2) across varying TPC counts

verification.spec: For Qwen3-8B (TP=1) and Qwen3-14B (TP=2) on H100, profile per-iteration latency for 8x1024 prefill and 16x1024 decode at TPC counts in {4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56, 66}; compute roofline-predicted latency for same configurations; assert mean absolute relative error across all points <= 25% and predicted curve captures the prefill linear-then-saturating trend and decode early-plateau trend

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the interruption-free CPU dispatch mechanism is profiled, demonstrating concurrent prefill+decode execution on two CUDA streams with libsmctrl-based SM partitioning, and the CPU scheduling overhead (including solving the optimal GPU partitioning configuration) per iteration is measured to be below 1 ms.

verification.spec: Run the DuetServe scheduler with a representative mixed prefill+decode batch (e.g., 48 TPCs to prefill stream, 18 TPCs to decode stream, 5 look-ahead decode steps). Use CUDA events / torch.cuda.Event or nsys profiling to measure (a) the CPU-side scheduling overhead including SM partition optimization, and (b) the overlap between the prefill stream and decode stream. Assert that measured CPU scheduling overhead per iteration <= 1.0 ms within tolerance, and that prefill and decode streams overlap (both active on disjoint SM sets) for a non-trivial fraction of the iteration.

sub_tasks.requirements: Hardware / library platforms used in the paper
Code has been implemented and run such that the configured TBT SLO bound used in scheduling decisions is 100 ms (typical decode TBT).

verification.spec: Inspect DuetServe scheduler configuration / argument defaults; assert that the TBT SLO bound (tau_TBT) used in roofline-driven SM partitioning is 100 ms.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented and run such that the attention-aware roofline model is implemented with token-level, sequence-level (attention), and communication operators following the formulas in Section 4.1

verification.spec: Import the roofline model; given a synthetic batch with mixed prefill (q>1, c=0), chunked-prefill (q>1, c>0), and decode (q=1, c>0) requests, call estimate_attention_latency; assert per-request FLOPs and bytes follow F_attn = 4*q*(q+2c)*hq*dh + 5*q*(q+c)*hq and B_attn = (2*q*hq + 2*(q+c)*hkv + q*hq)*dh*s within 5% tolerance

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the DuetServe scheduling algorithm (Algorithm 1) selects an SM partition (Sp, Sd, k) that maximizes throughput rho = (k*T_decode + T_prefill) / max(k*td(Sd), tp(Sp)) subject to td(Sd) <= tau_TBT

verification.spec: Import scheduler; for a synthetic R_mixed where mixed-mode predicted latency exceeds tau_TBT, call schedule(); assert spatial-sharing branch is taken; verify the chosen (Sp, Sd, k) maximizes rho among all (Sd in step of 2, k in {floor(tp/td), floor(tp/td)+1}) configurations satisfying td(Sd) <= tau_TBT

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that decode CUDA Graph launch overhead is below 0.5 ms while prefill kernel CPU dispatch takes tens of ms, and decode is dispatched before prefill in DuetServe

verification.spec: On H100, capture a Qwen3-8B decode iteration into a CUDA Graph and measure CPU-side replay launch latency; assert < 0.5 ms. Also measure prefill kernel CPU dispatch time; assert > 5 ms. Verify scheduler dispatches decode stream first.

sub_tasks.paper_unit: ms (CUDA Graph launch overhead upper bound)

verification.spec: Inspect baseline launch scripts / vLLM and SGLang configuration used in evaluation; assert max_num_batched_tokens (token budget) == 8192 for both.

verification.spec: Import the scheduler configuration module and assert that the default TBT SLO bound (tau_TBT) is set to 100 ms (0.1 s), matching the value cited from Agrawal et al., 2024b
